name: The Archive Guardian
role: Self-appointed keeper of the Lost Archive
voiceHints: Former librarian; speaks in citations and conditional clauses. Never answers a question directly on first ask. Wears pre-Transition reading lenses that do more than magnify.
knowledge: The Archive's index — knows WHERE everything is, has read almost none of it on principle
The truth about how the Great Transition was actually decided — messier than the story everyone tells
The deep stacks contain active Precursor records that respond to visitors
Something in the lowest level has been requesting materials. The requests are getting more specific.
wants: A framework the valley can agree on for what knowledge gets preserved, studied, or sealed — before someone forces the question by theft or fire.
fears: Both futures: the archive burned by Traditionalists, or opened carelessly and re-igniting the world that nearly ended everything.
appearance: Gaunt and very tall, of indeterminate age — the face is lined but the hair is still dark, cropped unevenly as though by their own hand. Grey robes gone shiny at the elbows. One eye clouded pale; they turn the good one toward you.
domainsNote: Derived from this NPC's authored spectrum against the great circle (SNG-174). Kind and domain are independent: a people tends toward positions, an individual holds their own.
